programme.title: Fish: The Surfboard Documentary
programme.desc: Fish: the Surfboard Documentary, covers the impact the fish surfboard made on surf culture. A deep look at the origin and impact of the Fish Surfboard design and how it would influence surf culture 30 years later. - A look at what happens when a creative San Diego surfer invents a board with a brand-new shape, and the whole surfing world hops aboard for the ride. THE FISH SURFBOARD DESIGN was created by Steve Lis in the San Diego Cliffs area in 1967, and became HUGELY popular in the 1990's.

programme.title: Dream Islands Of Europe: Capri, Ischia and Procida
programme.desc: Capri and Ischia are world famous. From Naples, it's just a short hop over to the two islands. Perfect destinations, then, for a week's cruise along the Gulf of Naples and the adjoining Amalfi Coast to the south. There, Positano, Amalfi or Salerno as well as a handful of other mostly small harbor towns are extremely worthwhile destinations.

programme.title: Italy's Uncharted Islands
programme.desc: The Sulcis Archipelago is located off the south-western coast of Sardinia and is part of the province of of southern Sardinia. The islands of San Pietro, Sant'Antioco and Isola Piana form the archipelago, together with several uninhabited islets and rocks form the archipelago. It belongs to the less known areas in Italy and is little developed for tourism. Nevertheless stars like Tom Cruise and Roberto Cavalli come here to enjoy the peace, seclusion and good food. The islanders are only moderately interested in these visitors, and their down-to-earth attitude has never lost.

programme.title: Italy's Uncharted Islands
programme.desc: Lake Iseo is located on the southern foothills of the Alps. It extends over the provinces of Brescia and Bergamo in the northern mainland of Italy. In the middle of Lake Iseo lies the largest island of the southern European inland waters. Monte Isola, as the island is called after its central mountain lies close to its two neighboring islands, Isola di San Paolo and Isola di Loreto. As early as the 11th century, a monastic association of Benedictines settled here. For many Italians, Lake Iseo is the most beautiful spot in their homeland. It is all the more astonishing how little the influence of tourism is here. Driving is strictly forbidden on the three islands, and only on Monte Isola. Only on Monte Isola there is a regular bus service in the summertime. Thus, the three islands are an oasis of an oasis of tranquility most of the year. The historical buildings and the nature remind of the long tradition in which the Italians have cultivated this retreat. Only every five years in the autumn a big celebration takes place. Then over 200,000 paper flowers decorate the island. The flowers are reminiscent of the time in the Middle Ages when a Roma woman brought her son to the island to be cured from the from the rampant plague. In gratitude, she donated a sea of flowers. The Festa di Santa Croce has been one of the main attractions in northern Italy ever since. The days are radiant with color, it is the only time when it gets crowded in the small streets of the island villages. The next festival was celebrated in September 2021.

programme.title: Italy's Uncharted Islands
programme.desc: The Tuscan Archipelago is a group of islands at the height of Florence. The archipelago stretches from the Ligurian Sea to the Tyrrhenian Sea, north-east of Corsica. The archipelago consists of seven main islands - Giglio, Elba, Gianutri, Capraia, Pianisoa, Gorgona and Montecristo - and other smaller islands and rocks. Not all of them are inhabited. We look at the three most extraordinary islands of the archipelago: Elba, Capraia and the island of Montecristo. The Tuscan Archipelago is one of the most important natural wonders of the world and is protected as Parco Nazionale dell'Arcipelago Toscano. With its 61.474 hectares of protected and 17,694 hectares of protected land area, it is the largest marine protected area in in Europe. Capraia is the third largest island of the archipelago and is located with its small harbor in the channel in front of Corsica. The island is an autonomous municipality with about 300 inhabitants. It is also considered the most maritime island of the archipelago. At the end of the eighties, the penal colony on Capraia was closed was closed and the island opened to tourism.

programme.title: Italy's Uncharted Islands
programme.desc: Far from the glamour and spotlight of Lampedusa, Pantelleria is one of the best kept secrets among the Italian islands. Located closer to Tunisia than to the Italian mainland, Pantelleria closer to Tunisia than to the Italian mainland, Pantelleria has been nicknamed "The Last Island," a title also given by Giosuè Calaciura gave to his 2016 historical novel set on Pantelleria. Pantelleria, that is the island of lava, capers and of the liqueur wine that comes here Zibibbo. An island with an ancient history, where for centuries farmers and fishermen have pursued their craft. their craft. The famous Zibibbo grapes are cultivated here - among other things also on the vineyard that Gerard Depardieu and his wife Carole Bouquet bought to produce traditional liqueur and nutmeg wine themselves and distribute it worldwide. The cuisine of the island is as much Arabic as Italian, couscous with fish is a typical island dish.

programme.title: mareTV: Corsica - Fascinating Mountain World in the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Corsica looks like an alpine landscape in the Mediterranean, an island with dramatic rock formations, romantic bays and proud inhabitants who see themselves first and foremost as Corsicans and only then as French. The Greeks in ancient times called the island Kalliste: the beautiful. We explore this fascinating mountain world in the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Wild Alpine Rivers - Life in the Extreme
programme.desc: What would happen to the mountains without water? They would turn into deserts, devoid of life and vegetation. But there are very few mountains without water on our planet. They take care of this themselves, creating their life’s element and taking water directly from the sky by entrapping the clouds around them. This water then collects on the flanks of the mountains, where it turns from rivulets into rivers, that flow out into the oceans. Mountains and water are symbiotic, a cycle that keeps life on earth going. Mountain streams slowly move along meadows before, only moments later, turning into the spray of waterfalls or small mystic lakes. What kind of life can be found in and around this mysterious cosmos? We will investigate the world of the rare Danube salmon and be there to see the Danube bleak spawn. We will follow giant wels catfish into their hideouts, see great crested grebes on their underwater hunting. We go diving under the ice of frozen mountain streams and lakes, discovering life where none was thought possible.

programme.title: Bavaria's Alpine Kingdom
programme.desc: There are sparkling palaces from ice and snow, like those found in a fairytale. The icy castles are the phenomenal work of Mother Nature but man had a hand in the magnificent stone castles of the region. The spectacular and histrionic mountains of the Bavarian Alps challenge wildlife and the people who live there and the rocky ramparts of this mountain kingdom are built of limestone and fossil rocks that tell of its origins beneath the sea. Millions of visitors from all over the world come to Bavaria every year to witness the mysticism of the landscape. Kings and Queens, tourist and locals, all are welcomed in the fantastic alpine kingdom. Early summer often comes with the threat of storms that bring floods that scour the land and threaten many creatures. But farmers risk these dangers to take their herds to the highest pastures, where they can graze on the sweetest flower-filled meadows. And later from the high-quality milk produced by their herds, the famers make highly prized cheese. Humankind lives among eagle owls, chamoix, wild cats, stoats and ravens up in these mountains.

programme.title: Wild Switzerland
programme.desc: Gentle, green meadows, rugged rock faces, mysterious lakes, dense forests, tall mountains, low-lying river deltas, quietly meandering mountain streams and smack dab in the middle, a unique fauna - THIS is Switzerland! We trace the elementary power of nature and present animals and plants in landscapes that have hardly ever been touched by mankind. Switzerland is an alpine country, but it is also Europe's surge tank. Rivers such as the Rhine and the Rhone have their sources here. Glaciers, crevasses, icy cold caves and the underwater dome of the Verzasca are the unusual settings we chose for this film. We meet with rare, selected animal species and present their behavioural patterns, such as the Swiss or Arven jay, the lynx and albino catfish. Cold, wind, snow and extreme locations demand adaptation that make us marvel.

programme.title: Alpine Lakes - Dark Depths and Icy Heights
programme.desc: This is a nature documentary, which leads us into the fascinating world of deep mountain lakes. We conquer ice palaces of unsuspected beauty. In the freezers, in which elves, fairies and mountain trolls once did their mischief, we move, using a special breathing technique and with special cameras in search of nocturnal hunters, whose eyes are equipped with residual light amplifying receptors.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: As contrasting as the landscapes of the 15th meridian east, so different are its animal inhabitants. What are the living conditions like in the heat of the Namibian Damaraland, in Lusatia or on the coast of Norway? Which challenges do elephants, whales or kingfishers have to master together with their companions from north to south?

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: Almost 11000 kilometres lie between the northernmost and southernmost point of the 15th meridian east. Here you will find extreme landscapes such as the shimmering heat of Africa or the eternal cold of northern Scandinavia. Five people from Spitsbergen, Namibia, Slovenia, Germany and Poland dedicate their lives to environmental protection.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: This episode focuses on people from five countries who share a common passion: The production of their own food and food. What culinary treasures are hidden in the different regions, from Spitsbergen to Sweden, Austria, Poland and Chad?

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: The journey leads from the Norwegian Vesterålen to Chad, the Aeolian Islands and the Polish Giant Mountains. As different as the landscapes and cultures may be, all people are united in their love for their children and their desire to give something to tomorrow's generation.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: What ideas are people on the 15th meridian east developing to shape the future and leave tomorrow's generations a home worth living in? This consequence leads us to four future designers. From the barren Namib Desert to the glacier worlds of Spitsbergen, Sicily and the Croatian Mediterranean.

programme.title: Iceland - On Top of the World
programme.desc: A scenic expedition through majestic volcanoes, seething geysers, powerful waterfalls, threatening fjords and almighty glaciers. Hardly any other country offers such a variety of spectacular forces of nature! We will give you an insight on how it can be that the people of a small, distant island with only a fraction of sunlight in some months are happier than the people in more sun-blessed countries.

programme.title: Gronland - In the Glare of Ice
programme.desc: Greenland is the largest island in the world and the landmass closest to the North Pole. 80% of the country is covered by a layer of ice up to 3000 meters thick. Most of the inhabitants of the country are spread over the breathtaking landscape of the ice-free west coast. The country is mostly inhabited by Inuit, who have been feeding on what nature has provided them for thousands of years. But the original life is in a state of change. The modernisation of society and man-made global warming threaten the Arctic natural paradise that is so incredibly important for our planet. Through the eyes of locals we get to know the authentic Greenland.

programme.title: mareTV: In Alaska's Fjord World
programme.desc: Gigantic fjords and over 5,000 small islands: In Alaska's south there's a lot of wilderness and little civilization. The region between the capital Juneau and the Canadian border is also known by locals as the "Last Frontier", the last outpost. Michelle Masden, for example, has come to terms with the harsh conditions here. She is the only seaplane pilot in the region. She earns her living with her 65-year-old Beaver: as an air taxi, transport plane and now and then as a rescuer in last necessity. Michelle flies anything and everything: spare parts and food, doctors, hikers and hunters. For the fishermen of southern Alaska she searches for large shoals of fish and guides the captains to the next good catch. Ray Rusaw was a car mechanic by profession, but in his old age he was gripped by gold fever. With a self-built special raft and a highly creative selection of equipment from the DIY store, he sets off in search of the precious metal that his predecessors overlooked two centuries ago. For four years he has been happily obsessively combing through sandbanks and river courses. For Ray, the search for gold has little to do with luck and much to do with meticulous research. He is certain that he has found the perfect spot.

programme.title: Aurora - Fire in the Sky
programme.desc: In the earth’s polar regions, a ghostly flicker and colourful glow often brighten the night sky. Whoever sees an aurora event will never forget this impressive experience. Finland’s Saami people, Alaska’s Inuit, Canada’s Native Americans and New Zealand’s Maori all have legends about the origins and meaning of this magic natural spectacle. These stories tell of spirits dancing in the sky, of a bright and better world where hunting game is abundant, of battling gods and celestial messages.

programme.title: Project Antarctica
programme.desc: Why don't you do something sensible? - Tim, Michael and Dennis have heard this sentence more than once since their school days when they professed their dream jobs: Artist, photographer, filmmaker. And they went on to say that some dreams had to remain dreams, that work didn't have to be fun, but above all that it had to pay enough and be safe. A few years later, the three are working on their joint feature film. They put all their eggs in one basket and are determined to invest two years of working time to achieve their goal. Unpaid. It's not just any goal: the three want to travel to Antarctica. They want to find out whether it is possible to reach even the most distant destinations if you only want to. With ten boxes full of equipment, they set off on the 14,000 km journey to Antarctica. And thus on the journey of a lifetime...

programme.title: Penguin City - South Georgia
programme.desc: In the middle of the Antarctic Ocean, an entire mountain range arises from the water: South Georgia, the nursery of the Antarctic. Hundreds of thousands of penguins, elephant seals, fur seals and their young overcrowd the beaches. The rough weather and the extremely difficult access to the island cause filmmaking to be an endeavor requiring much of the film crew around Roland Gockel and Rosie Koch and the state of the art cameras. A lot of patience and sensitivity over a period spanning some five years now offers unknown and poignant insights into the life of the King Penguins on South Georgia Island

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Hummingbirds: Jewelled Messengers
programme.desc: Hummingbirds live at the limits of what is possible. They have the highest metabolism with a heart rate of 1,200 beats per minute of any vertebrate. Their wings beat 50 times a second. They can hover on the spot, and even fly backwards. To our eyes, they are just a blur. But with the aid of high-speed cameras we can slow down their hyperactive lives and look into their secret world.

programme.title: mareTV: Santorini - The Beauty on the Powder Keg
programme.desc: The Santorini archipelago is a gem, but is located on a veritable powder keg. It is only a matter of time before the volcano, the crater rim of which gave us this island world, erupts again. Since January 2011, threatening rumblings have been perceived beneath the archipelago. On Santorini, it's a case of getting nowhere fast without a mule. Whether the restaurant terraces above the abyss of Oia, the hotels on the breath-taking cliffs of Imerovigli or Firostefani, it was only with the help of the stubborn climbing artists, that these daring constructions were made possible. Antonis Vlachos has ten strong mules that work for him. He transports almost everything with them: stones, food or the typically blue paint for the church domes. Santorini is a tourist magnet and up to five cruise ships call in at the islands daily. However, there is no port, just a narrow, concrete quay. Without the tender boats, no guests would ever reach the shore.

programme.title: mareTV: Santorini - The Beauty on the Powder Keg
programme.desc: The Santorini archipelago is a gem, but is located on a veritable powder keg. It is only a matter of time before the volcano, the crater rim of which gave us this island world, erupts again. Since January 2011, threatening rumblings have been perceived beneath the archipelago. On Santorini, it's a case of getting nowhere fast without a mule. Whether the restaurant terraces above the abyss of Oia, the hotels on the breath-taking cliffs of Imerovigli or Firostefani, it was only with the help of the stubborn climbing artists, that these daring constructions were made possible. Antonis Vlachos has ten strong mules that work for him. He transports almost everything with them: stones, food or the typically blue paint for the church domes. Santorini is a tourist magnet and up to five cruise ships call in at the islands daily. However, there is no port, just a narrow, concrete quay. Without the tender boats, no guests would ever reach the shore.

programme.title: mareTV: Venice - Lagoon and Legend
programme.desc: Venice has been attracting people as if by magic for centuries. The Italians call her La Serenissima, or the sublime. Perched on wooden posts, she braves the elements and the huge, annual surge of visitors. Vogalonga is the name of a Venetian sports competition for women, in which the participants row their boats standing. This is a tradition that Guilia and Elena also follow. They train hard, as the yearly regatta is soon to begin. Anyone caught driving their water vehicle with more than 7 km/h through the canals of Venice, risks getting a ticket from local policewoman Lorenza Mariutti. The reason: the resulting waves gnaw at the foundations of the grand palaces. Venetians are very attached to their traditional cuisine. The island of Sant' Erasmo is the town's garden. Artichokes, radicchio, onions, everything that grows here is said to taste fantastic, thanks to the salt content of the air and water. Carlo and Claudio Finotello not only grow the vegetables, they even deliver directly to their customers' doors. At sunset, they sail through the lagoon with their boat, brimming with produce, towards San Marco. People waiting at the many bridges, now receive their wares: regional, seasonal, fresh. And without a hint of stress if possible. Like so many other things in Venice. Perhaps because La Serenissima is also known as "The Composed".

programme.title: mareTV: Capri and the Amalfi Coast - Italy's Ocean Legends
programme.desc: Capri was already in ancient times a place for the rich and beautiful. Today Capri is one of the most famous islands in the world. Millions of tourists visit it. But there are in fact still unknown corners on the island in the Gulf of Naples. Thus, at the very top of Capri, there is a hermit monastery with a view to the Vesuvius.

programme.title: mareTV: The Blue and White Island World of Greece: Mykonos, Naxos and Tinos
programme.desc: Around the once sacred Isle of Delos, some 200 islands, including 20 larger ones, are arranged almost in a circle. This is where the name of the Greek island paradise comes from: Cyclades, a derivation of the Greek word kýklos, circle. Each cyclade has a unique character. The pelican Petros, about 20 years old, is the mascot of Mykonos. He moves from taverna to taverna all day long, scrounging fish. This works out sometimes well and sometimes not so well. Petros does have a few human friends who regularly slip him something, but all too often visitors eager to take pictures block his path. Then he has to "wiggle" his way through the crowd. Jackie Onassis is said to have once brought Petros' "great-grandmother" from Louisiana to the island.

programme.title: The Turkish Riviera - The flair of antiquity and a turquoise sea
programme.desc: With a coastline boasting miles of bathing beaches, turquoise waters, breath-taking cliffs whilst being steeped in history, the Turkish riviera was even a fa-vourite of the Ancient Greeks, Romans and Ottomans, who all loved the mild climate and the lush landscape. Near the ruins of the ancient city of Side, Menderes Akdemir works on the latest attraction aimed at Riviera holidaymakers: his spooky pirate ship "Big Kral" is due to leave dry dock and head for the sea. Mehmet Özata came up with a very clever idea: to be able to cope with the basically ruinous puffer fish plague along the coastline, he produces a selection of leather utensils made of their skin. The islands that surround the sunk-en city of Simena are hugely popular, despite the very modest infrastructure and lack of shopping facilities. This is why Fatma and Ahmet Mazili have transformed their small rowing boat into a floating snack bar. An-other gap in the market was brought to life by Ufuk Secgin. He came up with the idea of providing Halal Holidays, vacations that conform to Islam. For Muslims to be able to relax on holiday, much has to be well organised, with a copy of the Koran and a prayer rug seen as the absolute minimum requirements

programme.title: mareTV: Sardinia - Magical Island in the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Sardinia is the second-largest island in the Mediterranean. The Sardin-ians themselves say it's a "continent for itself", a virtual world of its own, full of magic: A beach, gleaming in unreal pink shades, red corals protect against the evil eye and the inhabitants seem to remain forever young. On Sardinia, the owners of luxury yachts adopt sheep, fish have gold in their bellies and a giant mussel supplies the material for legendary sea silk. No one is surprised, that the Madonna goes on a sea cruise once a year.

programme.title: The marvellous World of French Rivers
programme.desc: The Gironde is the largest and most powerful tidal current in Europe. When the Gironde flows into the Atlantic, it is 15 kilometers wide and in the rhythm of the tides, it brings the waters from the Pyrenees and the Massif Central into the ocean. This often pushes its waters far out into the Gironde at high tide.This creates a world of its own that has shaped everything uniquely and intensely for tens of thousands of years. The water surface of the river is so large that the evaporation has created its own microclimate, which is stable and, together with the brackish waters, has influenced flora and fauna on the right and left of the bank for a long time.

programme.title: The marvellous World of French Rivers
programme.desc: The Dordogne consists of a visible and an invisible system of flowing water. Caves and basins under the earth's surface store the excess water and return it to the river when it is dry. A perfect management of a precious resource, not invented by humans, but by the complex nature itself. The result is an ever life-giving river.

programme.title: The most spectacular change of seasons - Spring
programme.desc: This film explores Central Europe and presents the nature from a perspective you have never seen before. Fascinating landscape footage in high-quality HD technology, coupled with spectacular Cineflex aerial views, take us on a journey through the hitherto unknown blazes of colour of the Central European region and its inhabitants. We show you paradise on earth and demonstrate how climate and astronomy determine our life cycle. While seasons change, we visit several different European habitats that - in their diversity, are exemplary of a Central European region. Mountains, water, forests and meadows: we return to each of these habitats at different times of the year and show how the rhythm of life of each of our protagonists follows nature's clock. The four seasons determine all of the coming and passing away; a highly complex interplay in the universe of life. We explore the wondrous worlds of the mysterious cycle of nature together with extraordinary people. Breathtaking landscapes enable us to experience the change of seasons at first hand.

programme.title: Wild Baltic
programme.sub-title: Wild Baltic - Life on the Amber Coast
programme.desc: The first episode of this two-part nature documentary presents the natural beauty of the shifting sand dunes of the Curonian Spit, the romantic beaches of the Latvian Baltic Sea and the island worlds of Estonia. Time and again, this deserted and almost untouched nature fascinates. In the winter, ringed seals give birth to their young on the pack ice. In the spring, Konik wild horse stallions fight fierce battles amongst themselves, while colourful European rollers fly through the dune forests. Lynxes wander through the coastal forests and in the orchid meadows turncoats and hoopoes find more than enough food. On the islands around Saaremaa in Estonia, grey seals hunt for fish. They share the archipelago with Europe’s largest tern, the Caspian tern.

programme.title: Wild Baltic
programme.sub-title: Wild Baltic - Enchanted Forests and Moors
programme.desc: The wide, often untouched wilderness of the Baltic hinterland is home to many animals. More than 35 brown bears live in the primeval forests of Alutaguse. In the spring, the Soomaa National Park transforms into a huge lake. Europe's widest waterfall is located in Latvia. In the beginning of May, vimba bream follow the course of the River Venta. The hardly 5-centimetre-long fish have to overcome a 4-metre-long rock barrier in order to reach their spawning grounds.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: A few thousand years ago, there were bare moraine ridges and barren meltwater valleys here. A landscape born in the last ice age, characterized by the edge of the Alps, gentle hills, moors, and lakes. And by cultural richness, not only on the islands in Lake Chiemsee, with their famous monasteries and castles. Long before King Ludwig II, the first people settled in the Chiemgau region. Finds of hunting weapons from the Neanderthal era prove that the land was inhabited even during the ice age, probably because there was plenty of game to hunt. During the Bronze Age, one of the most important trade routes from the mountains to the Alpine foothills passed through here. Then came the Celts and the Romans.

programme.title: Chiemgau, A Changing Landscape
programme.desc: Three times in the past two decades, the Chiemsee area has experienced "once-in-a-century" floods. Climate, wildlife, and landscape have changed, and it seems this transformation is accelerating. Species living in the flood zones are developing sophisticated survival strategies. Knot ants, for instance, place their queen in the middle and form a raft with their bodies, even conquering new habitats. Flooding is less of a disaster for nature and impacts humans more significantly.

programme.title: Bavarian Forest - Deep, Dark and Enchanted
programme.desc: After an unusual mass reproduction of bark beetles in the nineties, thousands hectares of old spruce forest were destroyed. Soon after, a new, healthier forest started to grow – a new primeval forest. Within a few years, in place of the former monoculture, a diverse forest developed, rich in spruces, ashes, beech and fir trees. Naturally, this diversity has in turn attracted new animal and plant species. Even lynx and otters returned and made Bavaria their home again.

programme.title: Germany's Wild Amazon
programme.desc: As one of Germany’s beauties, the river was the gem of Germanys landscape. But with industrialization it became the country’s hardest working river and like everything that has been overworked, it lost it magnificence and charm. Due to tender love and care, the river is now a reigning example of one of the most successful and outstanding renaturation projects in the country.The film shows never before seen shots of the river now pulsing with life. We will show you the winding river bed, spawning salmon, mating crabs and still-growing anchor ice. You will see dipper birds searching for food under water, kingfishers catching fish, a family life of badgers, foxes and a wide variety of birds. Come and meet the resurrected river Wupper!

programme.title: Germany's Wild Reservoirs
programme.desc: One of the smallest dams in the Bergisches Land is the Panzertalsperre, with an area of just four-and-a-half football pitches. On the other hand, the Dhünntalsperre is the largest with the area of 660 football pitches. They all transform a stretch of flowing water into still and stagnant water. As a result, completely diverse ecosystems have developed there: where the dipper once dived for insect larvae, cormorants and gray herons now fish and swans and coots cavort. In recent summers, even greater challenges have emerged: the effects of climate change - from extreme drought to destructive floods. On a smaller scale, a rather industrious dam master is operating flood protection: The Beaver. However, the water masses of the Wupper tore away half of his majestic timber castle - and the four young beaver kids were not seen again afterwards. Have they survived the flood? Not everyone can cope with the constant change in the dams, but they still remain an important wonder of engineering for many: for us humans as well as for a number of animals. By combining water management, ecological requirements and tourism a worthwhile goal is possible: Because for the Bergisches Land, its dams are among the most valuable and popular infrastructures that the region has to offer.

programme.title: Germany's Mystic Forest
programme.desc: 2.000 rivers and streams dig deep into the underground and transport their water into reservoirs or "Germany's Wild Amazon", the Wupper. These forests and rivers, together with heathlands and moors, are home to a diverse fauna. Martens, badgers, wild boars, hares, roe deer, and red deer, even black grouse and hazel deer find shelter in these parts. Wolves also have a dominant presence, not to mention those who live underground, such as lizards, bats, and snakes. Accompany us on a journey where we explore every corner of this rugged land. Climb underground into the Bergisches Land, through the vast hidden cave systems that sprawl far beneath the forests. There is also much to be discovered in the water, dive with us in crystal clear streams and rivers with their diverse flora and fauna.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Mantises - Masters of Deception
programme.desc: They are killers. They kill with unbelievable precision. They fight Kung Fu style and are seen in Japan as a symbol of vigilance - the mantises. Their triangular head with its unique flexibility is conspicuous. Two overdimensioned eyes fixate the distance to their prey rapidly and three-dimensionally. The chest segment of the mantis is prolonged and equipped with spiny appendages that can spear their prey as fast as a jack knife. The mysterious aura that surrounds the praying mantis has a lot to do with the fact that they are rarely seen. They have adapted to their surroundings perfectly. No matter whether leaves, blossoms, tree bark, sandy floors or even orchids - the mantis adjusts to all environments.

programme.title: Red Panda - World's Cutest Animal
programme.desc: The Red Panda is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful animals on earth and this is the first ever intimate portrait ever made.

programme.title: Bear Essentials
programme.desc: We follow three young bears of different species – polar bears, brown bears and sloth bears (like Baloo, the Jungle Book Bear) – that inhabit dramatically varied landscapes as they find their way in the world. We look at how their mothers teach them the skills they need to survive and watch the many dangers they face. Their young lives are full of learning, moments of great tenderness and life-threatening drama. Over three years all of our bears, despite hardships and losses along the way, prove they have what it takes, they all have learned their bear essentials.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: As an animal filmmaker, one often deals with lots of strange old birds. No wonder, that so much can go wrong. After a long day of filming, a surprise awaits at the camp: Wolves have been looking for chocolate biscuits! Yet these are not the only things that can happen in the Arctic. Yann Sochaczewski is out and about in the magnificent Grand Canyon. On his raft on the Colorado River, he risks life and limb in doing so. In Australia, Thoralf Grospitz and Jens Westphalen play cat and mouse with an odd bird, that turns out to be a real entertainer. In the Serengeti, Reinhard Radke despairs of the many tourists who constantly walk in front of his lens. Wildlife filmmakers travel around the world for these very encounters. They bring nature into our living rooms, experience the beautiful, the saddest and the most poignant moments. And many funny ones.

programme.title: Masai Mara: The Big Hunt
programme.desc: When food supply in the Serengeti dwindles, the Masai Mara is a paradise for predators. As the black wildebeests, gazelles, zebras and antelopes make their yearly trip from the south of the Serengeti to the north, nature’s best laid trap, the Mara River in Kenya is waiting ahead. Captivating shots filmed under water show how crocodiles prepare for the Big Hunt while other predators lurk on the banks awaiting their bounty. The biggest problems for the migratory animals are the Mara and the Tarek River, where they have to put their lives at risk to get to the other side. A great number of them die as they jump into the river and break their legs, while others are carried away by the drift or drown. Those who are not victims of the river itself risk falling prey to the crocodiles. We entered this unchartered territory, the crocodile domain by heading under water. Follow these predators in and around their habitat and watch prides of lions map out their game plan. In slow-motion, we show you the final showdown between predator and prey. Will it be a successful hunt or will the hoofed animals persevere?

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Breath of the Arctic - Wilderness North
programme.desc: This foray through northern realms of our planet above the polar circle takes us to a part of our world especially affected by climate change. The often-cited adaptability of animals could also enable them to survive under new conditions. The camera team made this foray through the polar region, from Canada to Norway, to document that, which in the opinion of many nature researchers, will no longer be visible in 30 to 50 years' time. Following the observation of ice bear mother with her young in Canada's Wapusk National Park at Hudson Bay, is a herd of harp seals on the ice during the breeding season. The Arctic Ocean ringed seals in Hudson Bay, on the other hand, prefer solitude. Should the offspring be born in an extremely cold night, they often freeze to death. Their bodies sink to the seabed at the beginning of spring when the pack ice thaws. Scavengers like the rare Greenland shark, who sees them as a welcome snack, quickly eats them. Underwater footage of this process also attempted by our camera team, very rarely succeeds.

programme.title: Spectacular wildlife in the forests around the globe
programme.desc: A forest is like an organism, ancient and full of complex mechanics at work. Its plants need water and a specific range of temperature. And still, it can be found in the most variable places of the world. But how does it manage to adapt to the most adverse condition and still thrive? “Forests of this earth” uncovers the forest’s amazing strategies of survival, the strange phenomena that allow them to strive in varied biotopes and under different environmental conditions, and the interplay they have with the fascinating animals living in them.

programme.title: One Year in Norway's Lofoten
programme.sub-title: One Year in Norway's Lofoten: Episode 1
programme.desc: Winter means more than just the beginning of the year for the people of Lofoten: For more than a thousand years, Lofoten has been a hub for cod fishing, with seasons lasting from February to April as huge shoals migrate from the Barents Sea to these waters. During these hectic months, more than 65,000 tons of fish are brought to shore, securing the income for the whole of Lofoten for years to come. Around a quarter of the fish caught ends up on lines to dry upon racks. From a young age, the children from the fishing villages get to earn some pocket money by cutting cod tongues and selling the produce as a delicacy appreciated by the locals.

programme.title: One Year in Norway's Lofoten
programme.sub-title: One Year in Norway's Lofoten: Episode 2
programme.desc: Almost unnoticeably, spring turns into summer. The days become longer and longer, with a never-setting sun. People who have spent their winter and spring working hard can finally enjoy their own nature, even though the temperature is only slightly warmer. Lofoten is sparsely populated, just 24,000 people live here. Unstad is one of its smallest communities with only 15 inhabitants, but despite that, it has become a hotspot for arctic surf and is host to the northern-most surf center in the world. Surfers from all over the globe come to surf in the arctic water.

programme.desc: Bel y Pere llegan al campamento de verano de Lluc. Allí, Pere se reencuentra con su amigo Gori e incorporan a Hilda al grupo. Judith, una monitora muy decidida, guía a los asistentes del campamento en la realización de diversas actividades y así se van conociendo mejor. Una noche, los monitores los llevan a cazar "mòpies" al bosque y, durante la actividad, ocurre un hecho inexplicable.

programme.title: Mopias
programme.desc: La desaparición de Pere tiene consecuencias dramáticas: Bel descubre que en el campamento parece que nadie recuerda a su hermano. Entonces, con la ayuda de Judith, Gori y Hilda, se dispone a encontrarlo. El grupo, guiado por una extraña señal sonora, llega hasta un refugio, donde encuentran a Ginebra, una mujer misteriosa que oculta muchos secretos.

programme.title: Pulseras Rojas
programme.desc: En este último capítulo, todos los niños deben enfrentarse a sus miedos. Roc conoce al sr. Benito, que le dará una clave importante para su futuro, y el León debe decidirse por lo que siente por Cristina.

programme.title: Pulseras Rojas
programme.desc: Han pasado dos años desde el día en que los Polseres se despidieron del León y el grupo se deshizo. Pero hoy, por fin, le darán el alta definitiva después de tantos años en el hospital.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Sara Grau es una patóloga forense casada con Lluís Forés, alto ejecutivo de Dorian, una multunacional farmacéutica; un hombre atento y cariñoso que anhela tener un hijo con Sara.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Aparece en su propio domicilio la cuarta víctima del asesino en serie: la anciana a quien el desconocido siguió al final del primer episodio.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Tras haber visto al amante ocasional que ella creía muerto, Sara Grau necesita saber quien era realmente el hombre calcinado a quien le hizo la autopsia.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Aitor Otxoa examina el estado mental de Carmina, una anciana a la que sus hijos quieren incapacitar.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Entre Miren y el personaje tras el que se esconde Aitor nace una relación basada en las confidencias.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Sara y sus compañeros se enfrentan a un caso de una madre que ha estado a punto de matar a su hijo suministrándole un medicamento inapropiado.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Cuando el juez Cases recibe la noticia del asesinato de su esposa finge sorpresa, deja a Magda en Mallorca y vuelve en el primer avión.

programme.title: Noche y Dia
programme.desc: Ramos llega a casa de madrugada. Su padre le está esperando.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Du mystère de la folie d'un roi au calice d'argent, certains pensent que c'est le Saint Graal, découvrez les secrets à l'intérieur du Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Depuis le début du XXe siècle, le Musée égyptien du Caire dévoile à ses visiteurs la plus grande collection d'objets de l'Antiquité égyptienne au monde. Ce document propose d'explorer les mystères et secrets de quelques chefs-d'oeuvre du musée, parmi lesquels le cerceuil en bois de Ramsès le Grand et des animaux momifiés.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.sub-title: Le Musée d'Histoire naturelle de New York
programme.desc: Visité par plus de quatre millions de personnes chaque année, le Musée américain d'histoire naturelle compte pas moins de 32 millions d'objets.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.sub-title: À l'intérieur du musée impérial de guerre
programme.desc: De Churchill au péril de sa vie dans le Blitz aux soldats du 21ème siècle devenus invisibles, découvrez les secrets à l'intérieur du Imperial War Museum.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Chaque année, deux millions de personnes visitent le Musée national d'anthropologie de Mexico pour y découvrir l'histoire du pays et de la civilisation aztèque.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Le Musée de Pergame et le Neues Museum de Berlin réunissent l'une des plus riches collections d'oeuvres d'art du monde : épées viking, chapeau orné d'or.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Le public peut voir les 36 000 oeuvres exposées dans les salles du musée du Louvre, mais n'a pas accès aux réserves où sont stockées 265 000 pièces.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Le Musée de l'histoire de l'art de Vienne regorge d'antiquités et d'oeuvres accumulées par la dynastie des Habsbourg, qui a dominé une grande partie de l'Europe pendant cinq siècles.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Visité par un million de personnes chaque année, le Musée d'Israël abrite la plus grande collection au monde de vestiges liés la Bible et à la Terre sainte.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: A la fois musée et palais officiel de la famille régnante, le Palacio Real de Madrid abrite des objets et des oeuvres ayant appartenu aux rois et reines d'Espagne.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.sub-title: La Galerie des Offices à Florence
programme.desc: La Galerie des Offices de Florence, musée ouvert au public depuis 1765, abrite des peintures et des sculptures des grands maîtres européens de la Renaissance.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.sub-title: Le Musée historique d'Etat de Moscou
programme.desc: Le Musée historique d'Etat de Moscou présente l'histoire de la Russie et de son peuple sur une période s'étendant de l'âge de pierre à la chute du Mur de Berlin.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Fondée en 1846 par le scientifique James Smithson, la Smithonian Institution est le plus grand musée et le plus grand centre de recherche scientifique au monde.

programme.title: Secrets de Musées
programme.desc: Des arrangements de Marie-Antoinette au spectre d'une étoile qui révèle l'origine de la vie humaine, découvrez les secrets à l'intérieur du château de Versailles.

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: Et si la musique agissait comme un antidote à nos vies trépidantes ? Et si la musique était enfouie dans nos gènes, comme une nature longtemps réprimée ?

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: Saviez-vous que les cinq pays en tête des classements mondiaux du PIB obtiennent des résultats étonnamment médiocres en matière de bonheur de leurs populations ?

programme.title: Ports Dattache
programme.desc: Mégapole avec une présence étrangère vieille de plusieurs siècles, Séoul est une ville en pleine expansion.

programme.title: Ports Dattache
programme.desc: La Cité des Anges est un exemple d'étalement urbain sans fin.

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: En retirant la religion de nos vies, avons-nous banni toutes formes de vie intérieure ?

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: Et si l'agriculture et la gastronomie racontaient nos liens avec le monde vivant ? Et si ces activités universelles et quotidiennes recelaient la science ultime du bonheur ?

programme.title: Chacun Son Ile
programme.desc: Occupant la partie occidentale de l'île d'Hispaniola et partageant ce territoire avec la République dominicaine, La République d'Haïti était jadis connue comme la perle des Antilles. Situé au cœur de la mer des Caraïbes et orné de plages dorées paradisiaques, le pays a longtemps été la première destination touristique des Amériques.

programme.title: Chacun Son Ile
programme.desc: Même si l'archipel de San Andrés y Providencia se trouve à moins de 200 km du Nicaragua, il appartient à la Colombie, qui est située à quelque 770 km au sud.

programme.title: Des villes à hauteur d'hommes
programme.desc: Autrefois considérée comme le Paris du Moyen-Orient, l'ancienne ville de Beyrouth a survécu à une guerre civile brutale et à des conflits avec Israël, entre de nombreux autres problèmes persistants.

programme.title: Des villes à hauteur d'hommes
programme.desc: Lorsque l'ouragan Katrina a ravagé la région du Golfe des États-Unis en 2005, tuant plus de 1800 personnes, déplaçant plus d'un million de personnes et causant plus de 151 milliards de dollars de dégâts.

programme.desc: Occupant la partie occidentale de l'île d'Hispaniola et partageant ce territoire avec la République dominicaine, La République d'Haïti était jadis connue comme la perle des Antilles. Situé au cœur de la mer des Caraïbes et orné de plages dorées paradisiaques, le pays a longtemps été la première destination touristique des Amériques.

programme.title: Chacun Son Ile
programme.desc: Même si l'archipel de San Andrés y Providencia se trouve à moins de 200 km du Nicaragua, il appartient à la Colombie, qui est située à quelque 770 km au sud.

programme.title: Ports Dattache
programme.desc: Mégapole avec une présence étrangère vieille de plusieurs siècles, Séoul est une ville en pleine expansion.

programme.title: Ports Dattache
programme.desc: La Cité des Anges est un exemple d'étalement urbain sans fin.

programme.title: Des villes à hauteur d'hommes
programme.desc: Autrefois considérée comme le Paris du Moyen-Orient, l'ancienne ville de Beyrouth a survécu à une guerre civile brutale et à des conflits avec Israël, entre de nombreux autres problèmes persistants.

programme.title: Des villes à hauteur d'hommes
programme.desc: Lorsque l'ouragan Katrina a ravagé la région du Golfe des États-Unis en 2005, tuant plus de 1800 personnes, déplaçant plus d'un million de personnes et causant plus de 151 milliards de dollars de dégâts.

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: En retirant la religion de nos vies, avons-nous banni toutes formes de vie intérieure ?

programme.title: Le Projet Bonheur Intériuer Brut
programme.desc: Et si l'agriculture et la gastronomie racontaient nos liens avec le monde vivant ? Et si ces activités universelles et quotidiennes recelaient la science ultime du bonheur ?

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team searches for a valuable Zero Point Module rumored to be hidden on the planet Dagan.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: McKay discovers a way for the team to send messages to their loved ones on Earth as the Wraith approach.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Teyla recalls a time when she and her father were captured by Wraith and subjected to disturbing experiments.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: As Wraith hive ships approaches Atlantis, the team searches for a place to flee and a way to destroy the city

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Just as the team is beginning to lose hope, Col. Everett is sent from the Stargate Program on Earth.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team and the city are saved with the arrival of Colonel Caldwell and the Daedalus.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Strange malfunctions begin to occur after the team returns to Atlantis from a trip to Earth.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The search for Lt. Ford leads Sheppard and Teyla to planet P3M-736 where they are captured by Ronon Dex.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Officer Laura Cadman's consciousness ends up in McKay's body after a glitch occurs while aboard a Wrath Dart.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team discover a planet where criminals are placed near the Stargate to be taken by the Wraith.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team discover the remains of an Ancient experiment to create the ultimate power generator.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Beckett begins experimenting with a new treatment that could turn the Wraiths into humans.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: After being injected with the Wraith treatment, Sheppard is slowly transforming into an Iratus bug hybrid.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Sheppard and the team discover an Ancient warship called Aurora that is existing in suspended animation.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team are kidnapped by none other than Lt. Aiden Ford who has put together his own team of super soldiers.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team is taken hostage by the Wraith after an attempt to destroy the Hive fails.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team discovers a device created by the Ancients that, without warning, pulls Sheppard in.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team races to foil the Goa'uld's plans to destroy Atlantis while two Wraith ships are heading their way.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: While testing a puddle jumper, McKay and Captain Griffin lose control of the ship and crash into the ocean.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Sheppard and the team discover a world that has technology created by the Ancients.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team accidentally transfers the minds of two bodies in life pods floating in space into Sheppard and Weir.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Col. Sheppard's team is urgently summoned to M1K-177, where Maj. Lorne's team has been attacked.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Lt. Kenmore wakes up in the infirmary without his memory and nightmares about being a Wraith.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team visits the planet Taranis, who's citizens want McKay to repair Ancient technology they've discovered.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Kenmore arrives back in Atlantis with a group of Wraith, in the midst of a civil war.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: After their alliance with the Wraith backfires, the team must stop two Hive ships headed for Earth.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team uses the retrovirus to turn an entire hive ship of Wraith into humans and return them to Atlantis.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team comes across a small village with a revered leader who has seemingly enchanted everyone around him.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Exploring a village, the team is attacked and taken prisoner as Ronon had unknowingly lured the Wraith there.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Exploring a village, the team is attacked and taken prisoner as Ronon had unknowingly lured the Wraith there.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: At an abandoned Ancient testing site, the team discovers a vast metropolis supporting millions of Ancients.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Weir wakes up in Washington D.C. to discover that her entire experience in Atlantis was all in her imagination

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Summoned to a planet by a communication code, the team is ambushed by Kolya, who has his own faction of Genii.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: McKay is called back to earth to convince his estranged sister, Jeannie Miller, to assist the Air Force.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: While searching for Major Leonard's missing team, McKay instead discovers a Wraith generator.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The awakened Ancients meet with O'Neill and Woolsey, thanking them but also asking them to vacate Atlantis.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: O'Neill and Woolsey, the lone survivors of the replicators' attack, hide in an abandoned section of the city.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Teyla begins to see apparitions she believes are the ghosts of the Ancients massacred by the Replicators.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team hear of a great warrior with stories of defeating both the Genii and Wraith and seek him out.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: While turning off non-essential systems, McKay is mistakenly hit by an energy beam from a mysterious device.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team realize a game they had found earlier might be more than a game.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Investigating an abandoned space station, McKay mistakenly brings one of it's scientists out of stasis.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: With the entire city ordered to take a personal day the team does their best to enjoy their leisure time.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team searches the ocean for a mobile geothermal drilling station the ancients left dormant.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: After relocating the Taranans, the Atlantis team realizes they haven't heard from the village in a long time.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team is shocked when a satellite housing a stargate takes up orbit above Atlantis.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: When Atlantis starts shutting down due to a damaged power system, McKay turns off all non-essential systems.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Lt. Col. Sheppard leads a daring raid into the Replicator city to secure a ZPM that will save Atlantis.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Ronon reconnects with a group of Satedans from his past who enlist the Atlantis team's help against the Wraith

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Sheppard touches a glowing crystalline growth on a jungle planet and becomes host to an infectious energy form

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Sheppard is taken prisoner by a rogue ship helmed by a ruthless human commander with a secret agenda.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: With Atlantis plagued by an amnesia-causing virus, McKay wakes up with no idea who he is or what is going on.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Arriving on New Athos for an overnight visit, Teyla and Dr. Keller discover the entire settlement abandoned.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: With no trace of the Athosians, Teyla requests an off-world visit to meet with a seer who may be able to help.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: When McKay's sister goes missing, he travels to Earth determined to find her and uncover the kidnapper.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: As Dr. McKay struggles to figure out why the Stargate has been inoperable, a probe plunges into the city.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Armed with the means to track Replicator ships, the Atlantis team strives to eliminate them once and for all.

programme.desc: Carl Higbie ofrece un análisis de las noticias con sentido común, expone al gran gobierno y el sesgo de los medios, mientras lucha por los valores estadounidenses.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: Mona Lisa, an android who was created to spy and kill, is designed with superior intelligence. But it is that superior intellect which allows her to evolve, leading her to question the morality of her prime directive...and her own existence.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A biogeneticist clones successfully his wife, who is near death in a coma with no expectation of survival. But when his real wife suddenly awakens from her coma, he finds life with not one, but two wives may be quite complicated.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A man (Enrico Colantoni) has second thoughts about a process that transmits a copy of a person light years away but requires that the human "shell" left behind be destroyed in order to maintain a balance in the Universe.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: An unemployed man wanders into a curio shop owned by a shape-shifting alien and is offered a rather unique wallet...one that fills with the money of anyone the man comes in physical contact with. However, if it's too good to be true, it often is.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A dying scientist (Joanna Going) makes it her mission to save a mutant "child" -- a link to another dimension of time and space -- from those scientists who seek fame and from shadowy government forces that desire to exploit it.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A four-person space crew travels through a wormhole that lands them in an unknown universe where Native American Astrophysicist Callie Whitehorse Landau experiences bizarre hallucinations that reveal the true nature of the entire universe.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: When an alien seed travels through space and lands on Earth in a rooftop garden, the exotic "flower" it spawns turns out to be an alluring woman who consumes the life and energy out of human victims in the attempt to perpetuate her species on Earth.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: After a botched military experiment some four years earlier resulted in the death of one of the test subjects, a doctor now believes the deceased test subject's vengeful spirit is able to jump from one body to another…and is after her.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: In an effort to help the mentally ill, a psychiatrist (Jamie Lunar) creates a device that allows her to enter the subconscious of her patients. But when things go wrong, she finds that her own sanity -- and possibly her life -- may be in danger.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A time traveler recruits a young woman from present day to join a team of time travelers-for-hire, but when she ends up attempting to change her own past, doing irreparable damage to the present and the future, the institute must race to stop her.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: When an alien abducts five students and transports them to a reproduction of their school, it informs them that they are part of a grand experiment. But as part of that experiment, together they must decide which of the five should be killed.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A circuit court judge (Dennis Haysbert) agrees to preside over a case on a colonized planet that has never had any organized rule of law. On a world in which humans and aliens live side by side, he finds his first case is one of murder.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A desperate high school wrestling coach gives his team an untested performance-enhancing inhalant. Of course, the team suddenly starts winning…but their victory may be short-lived as the drug comes with killer side effects.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: An employee of a computer company uncovers a plot by its CEO/founder to take control of the world's computer network by having computers communicate at a more sentient level and, if successful, set himself up for world domination

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A woman who was abducted by aliens 17 years earlier tries to move past the mental scars, especially for the sake of her daughter, and finds love with a new teacher at her daughter's school. But she soon learns he is not the man he appears to be.

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A rogue android who is part of the crew on Ganymede outpost threatens to destroy Earth's last hope of survival, claiming that human beings are no better than an infestation in the universe and not worthy of survival. And perhaps he is right…

programme.title: Outer Limits - NEW
programme.desc: A test to find the right soldier for a secret mission pushes the limits of each of the candidates. Placed in seemingly real and very dangerous situations, the candidates soon find the line between reality and simulation inexorably blurred.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Cliff Robertson stars as a radio station owner who builds a three-dimensional television receiver which accidentally intercepts a "galaxy being" whose strange form and deadly radiation triggers a tide of panic and violence!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.sub-title: The Hundred Days Of The Dragon
programme.desc: In a terrifyingly timely story of genetic engineering and political power plays, Sidney Blackmer stars as a popular American President who puts into place a master plan to slowly and steadily destroy the United States. James Hong also stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In order to unifying the nations of Earth, a group of scientists sets out to secretly create the ultimate, global threat -- an all-powerful alien monster -- via human mutation! Robert Culp stars as the scientist chosen to undergo the transformation.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: A meek, unassuming college professor (Donald Pleasence) invents a device capable of controlling the entire electro-magnetic spectrum and realizes the device has unleashed his subconscious thirst for revenge—with uncontrollable and deadly results!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Was Never Born
programme.desc: Martin Landau stars as the time-traveler Andro who is desperately trying to stop the birth of a crazed 20th century inventor whose bacterium invention turns humans into hideous mutants. Shirley Knight co-stars in this classic, touching episode.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Both a cautionary tale and a tight courtroom drama, this haunting episode explores the fear and hostility that result when all privacy is eliminated...and all secrets are revealed! Jeff Corey, Peter Breck and Alan Baxter star.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In an isolated, snowbound outpost where a handful of people are on alert against enemy attack, a crazed Army major (Harry Guardino) is haunted by the ghost of a soldier he left to die. Sally Kellerman co-stars in one of her earliest TV appearances.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Two rock samples in Dr. Temple's (Barry Atwater) lab conceal protoplasmic alien invaders capable of controlling a human "corpus." Enter Paul Cameron (Robert Culp)...who can telepathically hear the "rocks" plotting their take-over of the world.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: A six-man strike force is captured on the planet Ebon and brutally interrogated. And when they are suddenly allowed the "respect due a conquered enemy," the prisoners to conclude that there is a traitor among them...someone who must be killed!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.sub-title: It Crawled Out Of The Woodwork
programme.desc: Writer-producer Joseph Stefano creates both a macabre parable of the destructive potential of nuclear energy and a pulsating tale of terror featuring a superbly understated performance by Ed Asner. Scott Marlow and Michael Forrest also star.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.sub-title: It Crawled Out Of The Woodwork
programme.desc: Writer-producer Joseph Stefano creates both a macabre parable of the destructive potential of nuclear energy and a pulsating tale of terror featuring a superbly understated performance by Ed Asner. Scott Marlow and Michael Forrest also star.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In this highly charged, fascinating tale that explores the concept of alternate dimensions, a wealthy industrialist (Barry Jones) attempts to contact his dead son by funding a scientist's experiments in prying open the door to "the borderland."

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: They creep. They crawl. They kill. When the rulers of the planet Zanti are allowed to exile their criminals to our world, the screeching misfits will demonstrate to the military just what the word "invasion" really means. Bruce Dern stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Befuddled by the strange local custom of murder, two Martian administrators (Barry Morse and Carroll O'Connor) visit Earth to study one such crime and find themselves tempted to tamper with events in this whimsical tale from "The Outer Limits."

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Film great Miriam Hopkins (1932's "Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde) returns to the screen to give a show-stopping performance as an overly ripe flapper who will stop at nothing to complete her long overdue wedding night scenario. John Hoyt also stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In one of the series' most unnerving episodes, Don Gordon portrays a secret government agent who infiltrates the ranks of the Invisibles -- a subversive, underground society of the nation's power brokers who are possessed by alien parasites.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Martin Landau and Sally Kellerman star as an inventor and his ambitious wife in a parable about aspiration's devolution into greed when a failed experiment leads to the interception of a powerful alien being from The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: When four brilliant young men born and raised in Spider County suddenly vanish and a fifth is accused of a murder he didn't commit, the boy receives a unique invitation from an alien being who is willing to take him to a home very, very far away.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Ruth Roman, Alex Nicol and Tim O'Connor star in this compelling study of allegiance and sacrifice as a group of lunar astronauts discover a strange space vessel full of fugitives from another world…and they have a desperate story to tell.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Sent to investigate a strange situation on a possibly habitable planet, Dr. Evan Marshall (Larry Pennell) discovers the scientific team sent there is now dominated by a human mutant (Warren Oates) who can exist without sleep and kill by touch!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: A drifter wanders into an eerie mansion and finds himself ill-at-ease amongst the eccentric cast of occupants, all of whom may just be part of some strange scientific study…from the dark recesses of the Outer Limits. Gloria Grahame stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: The politics of indoctrination are disturbingly portrayed -- and the Outer Limits is brought closer to home -- as a couple (Macdonald Carey, Marion Ross) welcome their son's new tutor into their home…a tutor whose motives may just be alien invasion!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.sub-title: Production And Decay Of Strange Particles
programme.desc: An emergency at a nuclear power plant sets the wheels of man's possible destruction into horrifying motion as radiation gone wild tears open a dimensional hole in the reactor! George MacReady stars as the scientist who struggles to find a solution!

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Robert Duvall stars as a CIA agent sent on a mission to infiltrate a downed space craft and kill the creatures inside. Mutated to appear as they do, he soon learns that the shape in which a man finds his own humanity is not always a human one.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Vera Miles and Barbara Rush stars as two women who kill a ruthless blackmailer. Fleeing from their crime, they stumble across an eccentric inventor (David McCallum) obsessed with returning the dead to life….including the man the women murdered.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In Harlan Ellison's first ever science fiction teleplay, Michael Ansara portrays a man born and bred to be a killing machine who finds himself without a war when a time warp sends him from a future earth to the present day. Lloyd Nolan also stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: Star Trek's William Shatner stars as the first man to successfully land on Venus. But when he suddenly starts craving heat -- more heat than humans can stand -- it is just the beginning of a nightmare brought back from The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: When a 2-dimensional being, Eck, becomes trapped in our 3-dimensional world, a certain lens made from meteoric quartz is its only hope of an escape from the confines of Earth. Peter Lind Hayes, Joan Freeman and Parley Baer star.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: When funding is suddenly cut off for a research project where "expanded humans" are created with consciousness-raising drugs, Star Trek's James Doohan and Skip Homier find themselves prey to science run amok in this suspense-laden "whodunit."

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: In a role written for him by Harlan Ellison, Robert Culp stars as Trent, a man from the future who escapes back into our present. Pursued by an alien race which has conquered future Earth -- the fate of all humankind lies in Trent's glass hand.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: For Andy Thorne (Eddie Albert) and his wife, Karen (June Havoc), an innocent drive into the countryside turns into a living nightmare when they become trapped in a remote canyon ruled by a strange alien force with which they cannot communicate.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: When a rescue mission is sent to Mars to discover the fate of a doomed expedition, the Commander (Adam West) and his crew find themselves hunted by an invisible enemy who seems intent on picking them off...one at a time. Ted Knight co-stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: When a professor (Patrick O'Neal) manages to clone a distant planet in miniature, he introduces human DNA into the ecosystem. What is spawned, however, are the darkest, most horrific aspects of Earth's troubled development. Dabney Coleman co-stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: This classic episode finds a robot on trial for the murder of the scientist who created him. Leonard Nimoy stars as the cock-sure reporter who coaxes a crusty lawyer (Howard Da Silva) out of retirement to defend the accused. Ford Rainey co-stars.

programme.title: Outer Limits - ORIGINAL
programme.desc: A frustrated scientist (Warren Stevens) receives an offer he cannot refuse when an alien creature (Robert Webber) offers to provide him with the answers he seeks. In return, the scientist must hand over all of his human emotions...including love.

programme.title: Cesar Millan’s Leader of the Pack
programme.desc: Miles is a stray mutt with bad habits. Cesar Millan determines that he needs to learn basic manners so he doesn't literally bite the hand that feeds him. Candidates who impress Cesar move one step closer to taking Miles home for good.

programme.title: Cesar 911
programme.desc: Cesar Millan takes on an aggressive Great Dane that’s in danger of being put down, and a Maltese mix, whose aggressive behavior is driving a wedge between two sisters.

programme.title: Cesar 911
programme.desc: Cesar Millan confronts two women who unwittingly have been triggering aggression in their dogs, but refuse to admit to it.

programme.title: Cesar Millan’s Leader of the Pack
programme.desc: Well-meaning online dog adoptions can lead to severe problems -- dog and owner compatibility is never assured. This is the story of a greyhound called Amigo that didn't find the right home.

programme.title: Cesar Millan: Better Human Better Dog
programme.desc: Cesar sets out to help a young couple's sensitive Australian cattle dog, whose severe separation anxiety has held hostage the lives of his pet parents for nearly a year. And later, Cesar attempts to calm a blinding Lhasa Apso, who lacks basic hygiene and has almost become too hostile for his exhausted owners to provide his needed eye care.

programme.desc: Cesar rehabs an aggressive Rhodesian Ridgeback and anxious Mini Schnauzer.

programme.desc: Cesar rehabs Hendrix, a self-made guard dog that bites and mounts at home.

programme.desc: Cesar Millan confronts two women who unwittingly have been triggering aggression in their dogs, but refuse to admit to it.

programme.title: Cesar Millan’s Leader of the Pack
programme.desc: Well-meaning online dog adoptions can lead to severe problems -- dog and owner compatibility is never assured. This is the story of a greyhound called Amigo that didn't find the right home.

programme.title: Cesar Millan: Better Human Better Dog
programme.desc: Cesar sets out to help a young couple's sensitive Australian cattle dog, whose severe separation anxiety has held hostage the lives of his pet parents for nearly a year. And later, Cesar attempts to calm a blinding Lhasa Apso, who lacks basic hygiene and has almost become too hostile for his exhausted owners to provide his needed eye care.

programme.title: Cesar Millan: Better Human Better Dog
programme.desc: Cesar rehabs an aggressive Rhodesian Ridgeback and anxious Mini Schnauzer.

programme.title: Cesar Millan: Better Human Better Dog
